name: Sharanga Bow
description: The Sharanga is a divine bow of immense power, primarily associated with the Hindu god Vishnu and his avatars, particularly Rama and Krishna. It is described as being incredibly strong and capable of firing arrows that never miss their target, possessing the ability to pierce through any obstacle and travel vast distances. The Sharanga is not merely a weapon; it is a symbol of divine authority, righteousness, and the power to uphold dharma. Its origins are often traced back to the cosmic ocean or divine creation, signifying its inherent connection to the universe's fundamental forces. The bow is often depicted as being ornate and beautifully crafted, reflecting its divine nature. It is said to produce a deafening sound when strung, capable of striking fear into the hearts of enemies. While associated with Vishnu's incarnations, the Sharanga's ownership can vary across different accounts. It represents the unwavering commitment to justice and the ability to overcome any adversity. The Sharanga embodies the power of focus, precision, and divine intervention in the cosmic struggle between good and evil, making it one of the most significant weapons in Hindu mythology.
subtitle: The Divine Bow of Vishnu
origin: Created from cosmic energy or gifted by a celestial being.
currentLocation: Believed to be in the celestial realms or guarded by divine entities.

keyMyths.title: Rama's Use of the Sharanga
keyMyths.description: Rama wielded the Sharanga in his battle against Ravana, using its divine power to defeat the demon king and restore dharma. Its arrows were instrumental in piercing Ravana's defenses and ultimately ending his reign of terror.

keyMyths.title: Krishna and the Sharanga
keyMyths.description: Krishna, as an avatar of Vishnu, is also associated with the Sharanga, although he more frequently uses the Sudarshana Chakra. Some texts mention his proficiency with the bow and its role in maintaining cosmic order.

sources.author: Valmiki
sources.description: The epic poem detailing Rama's life and his use of the Sharanga.
sources.text: The epic poem detailing Rama's life and his use of the Sharanga.
